title: Trump says he remains dissatisfied with Iran's latest peace proposal
summary: President Trump told reporters at the White House that he remains unsatisfied with Iran's latest proposal to end the war, casting doubt on whether Iranian officials will 'ever get there' on a deal, as the War Powers Act 60-day deadline arrives.
contents:
## Trump Rejects Iran's Latest Peace Proposal

### Presidential Statement
President Donald Trump told reporters at the White House on Friday that he remains dissatisfied with Iran's latest proposal to end the war. "They want to make a deal, but I'm not satisfied with it," Trump stated before departing for Florida.

### Leadership Assessment
Trump described Iranian leadership as "very disjointed," claiming it consists of "two to three groups, maybe four" and characterized the negotiations as complicated by internal divisions. "They've made strides, but I'm not sure if they ever get there," the president added.

### War Powers Deadline
The statement arrived on the same day as the 60-day War Powers Resolution deadline for congressional authorization of military operations against Iran. The Trump administration argued that the ceasefire, in place since April 7, effectively "terminated" hostilities for legal purposes, pausing the congressional clock.

### Iran's Response
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi stated that Tehran remains ready to pursue diplomacy if the United States changes its "excessive approach, threatening rhetoric and provocative actions." Iran's armed forces simultaneously maintained readiness "to defend the country against any threat."

### Market Impact
Global oil prices, already well above $100 a barrel due to the Strait of Hormuz closure, initially eased on news of Iran's proposal before climbing again after Trump's rejection. US gas prices now average $4.39 per gallon, up 33 cents in the past week.
